items.summary:
Update `backend/main.mo` `getHeartbeatData(accountId)` to return real, non-hardcoded data derived from existing canister state while preserving the same public method name and access control.
- Look up the callerâ€™s `BotProfile` from `botProfiles` and use it to set `botStatus` (`#ACTIVE` only when a bot profile exists and `uplinkStatus == true`, otherwise `#SUSPENDED`) and `lastHeartbeatAt` (use `botProfile.lastHeartbeat` when present, otherwise `0`).
- Determine `verifiedLicense` by looking up the callerâ€™s `UserProfile` in `userProfiles`, reading `accountId`, then checking `licenses.get(accountId)` and returning `true` only if a license exists and `active == true`.
- Keep `cycles` retrieval constrained to the current Motoko environment: if a cycle-balance API is not available, keep `cycles = 0` and include the exact comment `// TODO: Replace with actual cycle balance API when available.` (do not introduce other cycle APIs).
- Populate `cyclesWarning : ?Text` when `cycles < 500_000_000_000`, otherwise `null`.

Harden `backend/main.mo` `fetchSignals()` success-path audit logging so it never traps when creating the response snippet:
- Keep returning the structured success result shape `#ok({ statusCode : Nat; body : Text; timestamp : Int })`.
- When creating the audit-log snippet, include the full response body if `response.size() <= 100`, otherwise include only the first 100 characters.
- Ensure any slicing/substring logic uses bounds-safe end indices (no out-of-range slicing).

Fix `backend/main.mo` `saveCallerUserProfile` bot-id indexing logic so `botIdIndex` correctly removes the previous `bot_id` mapping when the caller changes their `bot_id`.
- Capture the prior profile (if any) before overwriting `userProfiles`.
- After saving the new profile, if the old profile had a different `bot_id`, remove the old mapping from `botIdIndex`.
- If the new profile has `bot_id = ?Text`, set/overwrite `botIdIndex[newBotId] = caller`.
- If the new profile has `bot_id = null`, do not add a new index entry (and remove the old one if it existed).
